key_insight: A coupled LSTM + physics-informed GNN can reconstruct nonlinear, history-dependent local stress fields by learning both loading path memory and spatial equilibrium.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast surrogate field prediction for heterogeneous microstructures, reducing finite-element cost while staying usable across mesh resolutions and element types.
method: LSTM encodes macroscopic stress–strain history into a compact hidden state.
Physics-informed GNN reconstructs spatially resolved stress fields on the microstructure graph.
Training uses a warm-up weighted loss that balances reconstruction error with a discrete divergence/equilibrium penalty.
evidence_signal: Trained on 10,000 non-proportional loading paths for a plate-with-a-hole elasto-plastic problem, the model reports about 1000× speedup over FE and 1.9% cumulative error on sequences twice as long as training.
watch_item: Generalization depends on the surrogate matching the underlying constitutive regime; performance may degrade for unseen material behavior, loading extremes, or if equilibrium penalties are misweighted.
paper_title: Non-linear mechanical field reconstruction coupling recurrent neural networks with physics-informed graph neural networks
